metadata.archetype_justification: Zappa is a CLI tool and library with no persistent database connections, no user-specific state, no write-back endpoints, and no deployed API surface of its own. All operations are stateless command executions that orchestrate AWS resources on behalf of users.
metadata.context: Python framework for deploying WSGI apps on AWS Lambda.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute infrastructure — CLI tool with no deployed workload

top_gaps.title: API Authentication
top_gaps.gap: No API authentication — CLI tool exposes no HTTP endpoints
top_gaps.impact: No API surface to protect; limits service exposure potential

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity Integration
top_gaps.gap: No centralized identity integration — uses AWS IAM credential chain only
top_gaps.impact: No unified access control beyond environment-level credentials

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: No SAST, dependency scanning, or container scanning in CI/CD
top_gaps.impact: Dependency vulnerabilities may reach published packages undetected

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith with core.py (3,866 LOC) god class and no module boundaries
top_gaps.impact: Limits independent testability, maintainability, and team autonomy

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (extensive README, CHANGELOG, docs/ directory, CONTRIBUTING.md, example/ with detailed usage guides)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Index Zappa's documentation to answer developer questions about configuration, deployment patterns, and version migration
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Convert README sections into structured chunks; generate embeddings for settings reference and event source documentation

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Trigger CI runs, check build status across Python 3.9-3.14 matrix, monitor coverage reports, and manage CD workflow
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose GitHub Actions API access to the agent; define trigger conditions for manual CD workflow dispatch

decomposition_strategy.current_state: Single Python package with tightly coupled modules. core.py (3,866 LOC god class) and cli.py (3,655 LOC) are heavily intertwined.

approaches.name: Strangler Fig (Parallel Track)
approaches.description: Extract handler as standalone package first, then progressively extract AWS service modules from core.py

approaches.name: Conditional / Adaptive
approaches.description: Keep monolithic package but refactor core.py into internal modules with clear interfaces

approaches.name: Big-Bang Rewrite
approaches.description: Rewrite as multi-package monorepo. Not recommended — high risk of breaking existing user base

evaluations.reason: Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility, sync is correct design
Surface-gated: no persistent state to back up

findings.title: No managed compute infrastructure
findings.description: Zappa is a CLI tool published to PyPI with no deployed compute infrastructure. No Lambda functions, ECS tasks, or EC2 instances run this tool as a service.
findings.gap: No managed compute — all execution happens locally or in ephemeral CI environments.
findings.recommendation: If a hosted deployment service is desired, consider ECS/Fargate or Lambda. For the current CLI use case, this gap is structural.

findings.title: No network security configuration
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation — CLI tool with no deployed network infrastructure.
findings.gap: No network security configuration — tool is not deployed in a VPC.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for a CLI tool. If deployed as a hosted service, private subnets with least-privilege security groups would be required.

findings.title: No API entry point
findings.description: Zappa exposes no HTTP API — it is CLI-only. No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront serves as an entry point.
findings.gap: No API entry point — tool is CLI-only.
findings.recommendation: If a programmatic API is desired, expose deployment operations via API Gateway with authentication and throttling.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling — Zappa has no deployed compute to scale. It runs as a local CLI process.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling — no workload to scale.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for a CLI tool. No action needed.

findings.title: No IaC for own infrastructure
findings.description: No IaC defines Zappa's own operational infrastructure. GitHub Actions workflows exist but no Terraform/CDK manages repository settings, secrets, or branch protections.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage for the project's own infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Consider Terraform GitHub provider to codify repository settings, branch protection rules, and environment/secret management.

findings.title: Tightly-coupled monolith
findings.description: Single Python package with no module boundaries. core.py (3,866 LOC) is a god class. cli.py (3,655 LOC) directly instantiates and orchestrates core.py with pervasive cross-module coupling.
findings.gap: No module boundaries, no clear interfaces, god class pattern. Single deployable unit with no separation of concerns.
findings.recommendation: Refactor into bounded modules: separate Lambda handler runtime as independent package, extract AWS service adapters from core.py into focused modules, define clean interfaces between CLI and orchestration layers.

findings.title: No API versioning strategy
findings.description: No API versioning — CLI tool with no HTTP API surface. Settings format (zappa_settings.json) has no explicit version field for schema evolution.
findings.gap: No API versioning — settings format changes handled implicitly via deprecation warnings.
findings.recommendation: Add a settings schema version field to zappa_settings.json to enable forward-compatible configuration parsing.

findings.title: Environment variables for endpoints only
findings.description: Uses environment variables for AWS endpoint configuration via boto3. boto3 handles AWS service endpoint resolution internally. No dynamic service discovery needed for a CLI tool.
findings.gap: No dynamic service discovery, but boto3 endpoint resolution is adequate for the use case.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. boto3's endpoint resolution is the correct pattern for AWS SDK-based tools.

findings.title: S3 usage without parsing pipeline
findings.description: Zappa uses S3 for deployment package storage (ZIP uploads) but has no parsing or extraction pipeline for the stored data.
findings.gap: Data in S3 but no automated parsing or extraction pipeline.
findings.recommendation: For deployment tool use case, no parsing pipeline is needed. If deployment analytics are desired, consider S3 event-driven processing.

findings.title: No API authentication
findings.description: No API authentication — Zappa is a CLI tool with no HTTP endpoints. Authentication to AWS is handled by the boto3 credential chain.
findings.gap: No API authentication — no API surface exists to protect.
findings.recommendation: If a programmatic API is exposed in the future, implement OAuth2/JWT authentication. For CLI use case, boto3 credential chain is appropriate.

findings.title: No centralized identity integration
findings.description: No centralized identity integration. Relies on AWS IAM credential chain for all authentication. No Cognito, Okta, or other IdP integration.
findings.gap: No centralized identity — relies entirely on local AWS credential configuration.
findings.recommendation: For a CLI tool, AWS IAM credential chain with SSO support (aws sso login) is standard. Document AWS SSO/IAM Identity Center integration in README.

findings.title: Credentials in environment variables without rotation
findings.description: No plaintext credentials in source. AWS credentials handled via boto3 credential chain and GitHub Secrets with OIDC for PyPI. However, no Secrets Manager integration and no rotation configured.
findings.gap: Production credentials in environment variables without rotation. No Secrets Manager integration.
findings.recommendation: Document credential rotation best practices. Consider supporting AWS Secrets Manager for secret retrieval. Encourage IAM roles and SSO over long-lived access keys.

findings.title: No compute hardening or patching
findings.description: No compute hardening — Zappa has no deployed compute to harden. Tool runs on developer machines and GitHub-hosted CI runners (auto-patched).
findings.gap: No compute hardening — no deployed workload to harden.
findings.recommendation: For CI, GitHub-hosted runners are auto-patched. Add dependency vulnerability scanning for the package itself.

findings.title: No application security pipeline
findings.description: No SAST, DAST, or dependency vulnerability scanning in CI/CD. Pipeline has lint checks but no security-focused scanning. No Dependabot, pip-audit, safety, Bandit, or Semgrep.
findings.gap: No security scanning — zero security validation steps in pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot for dependency updates. Integrate pip-audit or safety for vulnerability detection. Add Bandit or Semgrep for Python SAST. Configure blocking gates on critical findings.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No tracing instrumented in the Zappa CLI tool itself. While it configures X-Ray for deployed apps, the tool has no tracing for its own deployment operations.
findings.gap: No tracing of deployment operations. Complex multi-step deployments have no end-to-end trace visibility.
findings.recommendation: Consider OpenTelemetry instrumentation for core.py to trace deployment operations and identify AWS API bottlenecks.

findings.title: No business metrics
findings.description: No custom metrics published. Deployment success rates, package sizes, and deployment durations are not tracked.
findings.gap: No business metrics — deployment operations are not measured or tracked.
findings.recommendation: Consider publishing deployment telemetry (success/failure rate, duration, package size) to inform tool improvement priorities.

findings.title: No anomaly detection or alerting
findings.description: No alerting configured — no deployed service to monitor.
findings.gap: No alerting — no production service to alert on.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for a CLI tool. If deployment metrics are added, alerting on failure rate spikes would be valuable.

findings.title: No incident response automation
findings.description: No runbooks, automated remediation, or incident workflows. Ad hoc troubleshooting only.
findings.gap: No runbooks or incident response automation.
findings.recommendation: Add structured troubleshooting guides for common deployment failures (CloudFormation rollback, Lambda size limits, IAM permission errors).

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No observability ownership defined. No CODEOWNERS for monitoring, no dashboards, no team-attributed alarms.
findings.gap: No observability ownership — no production service with monitoring needs.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for a CLI tool. If deployed as a hosted service, define CODEOWNERS for observability assets.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No resource tagging governance for own infrastructure (none exists). Zappa supports user-configurable tags for deployed resources but has no tagged AWS resources itself.
findings.gap: No tagging governance — no AWS resources owned by this repo.
findings.recommendation: No action needed for the tool itself. Document tagging best practices for users configuring zappa_settings.json.

triggering_questions.note: Tightly-coupled monolith with god class pattern

immediate_actions: Extract handler.py + wsgi.py + asgi.py as standalone lightweight package
Define clean interfaces between CLI, orchestration, and handler layers

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 but no deployed workload exists — CLI tool published to PyPI, not a running service. Contextual guard prevents triggering.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold but contextual guard blocks: no deployed workload to containerize

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Zappa is a CLI tool published to PyPI. There is no running service to containerize — compute is Lambda-based when users deploy via Zappa. The contextual guard 'must be EC2/VM-based' prevents this pathway.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); no commercial database engines detected

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures — trigger condition not met (requires < 3)

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 Not Evaluated (no database); DATA-Q3=4 (no database to version)

triggering_questions.note: No database engines to manage — trigger condition not met

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No database exists in this repository. INF-Q2 was not evaluated due to surface gating (has_persistent_data_store=false).

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A); no data processing workloads exist
pathways.not_triggered_reason: INF-Q4 was Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A for stateless-utility). No data processing workloads exist in this repository.

triggering_questions.note: No IaC defines project's own operational infrastructure

immediate_actions: Add Dependabot for dependency vulnerability scanning
Consider Terraform for GitHub repo settings

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context 'Python framework for deploying WSGI apps on AWS Lambda' contains no AI-related signal terms.
